vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1799: Codex-harness work agent spawns die within seconds of boot; pan start then hangs indefinitely waiting on the dead session

plan.title: Codex-harness work agent spawns die within seconds of boot; pan start then hangs indefinitely waiting on the dead session
plan.author: agent:claude-fable-5

references.label: PRD draft (verified file/line references, FR/NFR definitions)
PAN-1803 (adjacent codex work-tui pipeline — do not duplicate)

narratives.Problem: Codex work agents died seconds after boot and pan start reported success then hung >20 min. The boot-death root causes (codex exec '-s workspace' rejection; exec-mode design) were hotfixed on main same-day (163e63c01, eb79b04cf, 5e05abc06, b0a223c72, 31760795c, 9857b1ac1). What remains: (1) pan start prints unqualified success and exits 0 even when the session died and kickoff never landed — recordKickoffDeliveryFailure (agents.ts:1707) leaves status 'running' and spawnAgent returns normally; (2) waitForReadySignal (agents.ts:783) polls the full timeout against a dead session and deliverInitialPromptWithRetry retries delivery to a session known to be dead; (3) the pan start CLI process can hang after the summary on undrained event-loop handles — no explicit exit; (4) boot deaths leave zero diagnostics because the pane dies with the session (the incident took 4 blind hotfixes to root-cause); (5) work-tui codex agents never get a codex-thread-id (spawnRun's capture explicitly skips work-tui, agents.ts:3311), so memory transcript capture and session-id resolution return null.
narratives.Proposal: Make the spawn path fail fast and truthfully: detect dead sessions within one poll interval in every readiness wait; have spawnAgent mark state stopped and throw a typed AgentBootError on boot death (while preserving the alive-but-undelivered ghost lifecycle); capture the dead pane's tail via a bounded tmux remain-on-exit boot window into boot-failure.log; make pan start exit 1 with the diagnostic on boot death and terminate with explicit process.exit(0) on success; resolve the codex thread-id lazily from the per-agent rollout directory with write-back.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Re-implementing or modifying the codex boot fixes already on main (sandbox translation, work-tui mode, config.toml permission seeding, readiness detection, kickoff pointer file)
- Anything in feature/pan-1803's pending diff: kickoff file relocation to the agent dir, --skip-git-repo-check removal, codexHome path via getAgentDir (PAN-1803 owns these)
- Conversation-view rollout resolution (PAN-1805), conversation status tracking (PAN-1789), pi handoff delivery (PAN-1793), provider/role harness defaults (PAN-1787)
- Context-preserving resume for work-tui codex agents (work-tui launcher ignores resumeSessionId — follow-up issue, recorded as hazard H5)
- Changes to deacon orphan-patrol or auto-resume policy outside the boot window
- Changes to the exec-mode codex specialist spawn path (spawnRun)

autoDecisions.summary: Scoped the issue to the remaining fail-fast/diagnostics/truthfulness/thread-id gaps, treating the same-day hotfixes on origin/main as the boot-death fix.
autoDecisions.rationale: Verified by commit archaeology: 163e63c01, eb79b04cf, 5e05abc06, b0a223c72, 31760795c, 9857b1ac1 are all ancestors of origin/main; re-doing them would duplicate landed work and collide with PAN-1803's open branch.

autoDecisions.summary: spawnAgent throws a typed AgentBootError on dead-session boot failure (after marking state stopped); kickoff-failed-but-alive continues to return state with kickoffDelivered=false.
autoDecisions.rationale: A throw propagates truthfully through both callers (pan start catch → spinner.fail + exit 1; dashboard route → non-2xx) without new plumbing, while preserving the deacon ghost-lifecycle redelivery contract locked by existing integration tests.

autoDecisions.summary: Boot diagnostics via a bounded tmux remain-on-exit window (capture dead pane tail, then kill) instead of redirecting harness stderr to a file.
autoDecisions.rationale: TUIs may render on stderr; redirection risks breaking boot. remain-on-exit preserves exactly what a human attaching would have seen, and the window is closed right after readiness so post-boot death semantics are untouched.

autoDecisions.summary: Codex thread-id resolution is lazy (on-demand scan of per-agent codex-home/sessions with write-back) rather than a background poller at spawn time.
autoDecisions.rationale: A post-spawn poller holds the CLI event loop open — the same class of hang this issue exists to eliminate. Per-agent CODEX_HOME isolation makes newest-rollout unambiguous.

autoDecisions.summary: pan start ends with explicit process.exit(0) without flushing the fire-and-forget tracker transition.
autoDecisions.rationale: Established CLI pattern in this repo (close.ts:217, health.ts:175, dev.ts:678); the transition is best-effort, catch-logged, and has had the entire boot duration to complete.

items.title: Detect dead tmux sessions early in readiness waits and kickoff delivery

narrative.Action: In src/lib/agents.ts: (1) add a sessionExists(agentId) check to each iteration of waitForReadySignal (lines 783-804) so it returns false within one poll interval when the session is gone — mirroring the existing bail in waitForCodexTuiReady at line 262; (2) in deliverInitialPromptWithRetry (lines 1614-1671), when readiness fails because the session no longer exists, set lastFailure to 'session-died' (distinct from 'ready-signal-timeout') and break out of the retry loop instead of attempting a second delivery to a dead session. waitForPromptReady (line 288) is the shared dispatch — thread a way to distinguish 'not ready' from 'session gone' (e.g. return 'ready' | 'timeout' | 'died'), updating the three other waitForPromptReady call sites (agents.ts:3337, 4290, 4885) to treat 'died' as not-ready exactly as before. Tests: extend tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts with fake timers (vi.useFakeTimers + advanceTimersByTimeAsync) — never real delays.

items.title: Given a tmux session that no longer exists, waitForReadySignal returns false within one poll interval (~1s of fake time) instead of polling the full 30s timeout
When the session is gone, deliverInitialPromptWithRetry returns a failure containing 'session-died' and performs no second delivery attempt
A live-but-not-ready session still produces 'ready-signal-timeout' after 2 attempts — existing kickoff-timeout tests in tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts pass unchanged
spawnAgent boot-outcome contract: throw AgentBootError on dead session, preserve ghost lifecycle when alive

narrative.Action: In src/lib/agents.ts: define an exported AgentBootError class (fields: agentId, harness, phase: 'boot-died' | 'kickoff-failed', diagnostic?: string). In spawnAgent's kickoff-failure paths (the pi catch at lines 3667-3673 and the delivery-failed branch at lines 3684-3687), after recordKickoffDeliveryFailure resolves, check sessionExists(agentId): if the session is GONE, mark the agent state stopped (markAgentStoppedState + save) and throw AgentBootError(phase 'boot-died'); if the session is ALIVE, keep the existing behavior exactly (return state with status 'running', kickoffDelivered false — the deacon ghost-lifecycle contract locked by tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts:357-512). Also check sessionExists immediately before the markAgentRunning(state) at line 3691 so a session that died after a seemingly-ok delivery is caught. State must be stopped BEFORE the throw so the dashboard start route (which calls spawnAgent) never leaves a phantom 'running' record. Unit/integration tests with fake timers for both branches.

items.title: When the tmux session is dead after kickoff delivery fails, spawnAgent persists agent state status 'stopped' and throws AgentBootError carrying agentId, harness, and phase 'boot-died'

When kickoff delivery fails but the session is alive, spawnAgent returns state with status 'running' and kickoffDelivered false — existing ghost-lifecycle tests (agent-spawning.test.ts:357-512) pass unchanged
When the session dies after delivery succeeded but before markAgentRunning, spawnAgent throws AgentBootError instead of persisting a 'running' state
The dashboard start-agent route returns a non-2xx response with the AgentBootError message when spawnAgent throws, and the persisted agent state reads 'stopped'
Capture boot-death diagnostics via a bounded tmux remain-on-exit window

narrative.Action: In spawnAgent (src/lib/agents.ts:3637-3649): create the work-agent session with tmux remain-on-exit ON (use setOption from src/lib/tmux.ts right after createSession — planning sessions already use remain-on-exit, see spawn-planning-session.ts for the pattern). After readiness/delivery succeeds, set remain-on-exit OFF so post-boot deaths still vanish and the deacon orphan patrol semantics are unchanged (NFR-3, hazard H2). In the boot-death branch (spawn-boot-contract's 'boot-died' path), before killing anything: capturePane the dead pane's last ~60 lines, write them to <agentDir>/boot-failure.log, embed the last ~10 lines in the AgentBootError diagnostic, then killSession to remove the remained session. If capture fails (pane truly gone), proceed with a 'no pane output captured' diagnostic — capture is best-effort and must never block the throw. Tests with fake timers: dead-pane capture writes the log and the error contains the tail; success path flips remain-on-exit off and writes no log.

items.title: spawnAgent creates work-agent sessions with remain-on-exit on and applies remain-on-exit off after kickoff delivery succeeds
On boot death, spawnAgent persists the dead pane's captured tail to ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/boot-failure.log, records the tail in the AgentBootError message, and closes the remained session
When pane capture fails, spawnAgent still throws AgentBootError with a 'no pane output captured' diagnostic and still kills the remained session
Given a successful boot, spawnAgent produces no boot-failure.log and the session ends up with remain-on-exit off, so later deaths surface to the deacon exactly as before
pan start reports boot outcomes truthfully and terminates deterministically

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/start.ts issueCommand: (1) AgentBootError now propagates to the existing catch (line ~1170) which already does spinner.fail + process.exit(1) — ensure the boot diagnostic (pane tail) is printed in full, not just the error message's first line; (2) when spawnAgent returns a state with kickoffDelivered === false (alive-but-undelivered ghost), replace the unqualified '✔ Agent spawned' success block with spinner.warn plus an explicit note that the kickoff was not delivered and the deacon will redeliver on resume (mention pan logs <id> and pan tell <id>); (3) append explicit process.exit(0) as the final statement of the success path so undrained event-loop handles (fire-and-forget tracker HTTP, TLDR daemon exec, Effect runtime) can never hang the CLI after the summary — precedent: close.ts:217, health.ts:175, dev.ts:678. Live UAT (test role): pan start a scratch codex agent (gpt-5.5) — the agent survives at least 2 minutes with the kickoff pointer visible in the TUI and pan start exits 0 within seconds of printing the summary; a forced mid-boot session kill makes pan start exit 1 with the captured diagnostic.

items.title: When the spawned session dies during boot, pan start prints the boot diagnostic and exits with code 1 — no '✔ Agent spawned' success line appears
When the session is alive but kickoff delivery failed, pan start prints an explicit kickoff-failure warning naming the deacon redelivery path instead of the unqualified success block
On success, the pan start process exits with code 0 within seconds of printing the agent summary (explicit process.exit(0))

Live UAT: a codex work agent spawned via pan start survives at least 2 minutes with the kickoff visible in its TUI and pan start exits promptly; killing the session mid-boot produces exit code 1 with the captured pane diagnostic
Lazy codex thread-id resolution with write-back for work-tui agents

narrative.Action: In src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts: add resolveCodexThreadId(agentId): string | null — read the codex-thread-id file (threadIdPath, codex.ts:56); when absent or empty, scan the per-agent rollout directory <agentDir>/codex-home/sessions/ recursively for the newest rollout-*.jsonl by mtime (reuse the traversal pattern of collectRollouts, codex.ts:585-621), extract the id via extractThreadIdFromRollout (codex.ts:310), write it back with writeThreadId (codex.ts:76), and return it; return null when no rollout exists (agent never booted). Wire it into: (1) getLatestSessionIdSync fallback #4 in src/lib/agents.ts:2387-2396 (replace the direct file read), and (2) readCodexThreadId in src/lib/memory/transcript-source.ts:350-357 (so CodexTranscriptSource.resolveAgentTranscript stops returning null for work-tui agents whose spawn never captured a thread-id). Do NOT add any wait/poll at spawn time — resolution is strictly on-demand (decision D4). Unit tests: file present, file absent + rollout present (write-back happens), neither present (null); transcript-source test that a running codex work agent with a rollout but no thread-id file produces a TranscriptEntry.

items.title: resolveCodexThreadId returns the saved codex-thread-id when the file exists, without touching the rollout directory
With no codex-thread-id file but an existing rollout, resolveCodexThreadId extracts the id from the newest rollout, persists it to codex-thread-id, and returns it; with neither, it returns null
getLatestSessionIdSync resolves a session id for a work-tui codex agent whose spawn never wrote codex-thread-id
CodexTranscriptSource.resolveAgentTranscript returns a TranscriptEntry (not null) for a running codex work agent with a rollout but no pre-existing codex-thread-id file
End-to-end integration coverage for the boot-death and ghost-kickoff flows

narrative.Action: Extend tests/integration/agent-spawning.test.ts (existing harness mocks tmux via exactPaneTarget/sessionExists mocks — follow the established pattern at lines 216-512) with the full boot-death scenario: spawn a codex work-tui agent whose mocked session disappears before readiness; assert spawnAgent throws AgentBootError, the persisted state is 'stopped', the boot-failure.log write was attempted with the mocked pane tail, and the dead-session bail consumed only ~1 poll interval of fake time (not the full 60s of two timeout rounds). Re-assert the alive-but-undelivered ghost lifecycle suite (lines 357-512) is green. ALL timing via vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync() — repo rule, no real delays, no maxForks changes.

items.title: Integration test: when a codex work-tui spawn's session disappears before readiness, spawnAgent throws AgentBootError, persists state 'stopped', and records the mocked pane tail in the boot diagnostic
Integration test: the dead-session path returns within roughly one poll interval of fake time, not the full two 30s readiness rounds
The pre-existing ghost-lifecycle suite (kickoff failure with live session, resume redelivery) passes unchanged, and every new test covers its timing with fake timers exclusively
